dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI had the cleanest favorable signal on the front page: Codex was praised as a cheaper, quicker implementation tool in the Claude Code workflow thread, and GPT was singled out for a 0% false-positive rate in the binary-audit discussion, giving it direct positive mentions without a matching front-page backlash [E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI won because its mentions were both directly positive and spread across two separate discussions. In the Claude Code thread, a commenter said Codex moved quicker and avoided the context stall-outs they had seen with Claude Code while fitting into a cheaper plan, and in the binary-audit thread GPT was described as impressive for keeping false positives at zero even if recall was limited [E3][E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex got a detailed endorsement as a cheaper, faster implementer with fewer stall-outs than Claude Code, and GPT was called impressive for maintaining a 0% false-positive rate in the binary-audit thread even with limited detection depth [E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code drew substantial hands-on workflow discussion, but a prominent comparison said Codex and Gemini were cheaper and quicker with fewer context stalls, while Claude Opus 4.6's backdoor-detection result was paired with a high false-positive rate [E1][E2].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Gemini got one favorable comparison on price and speed in a coding-workflow thread, but that was outweighed by the backlash over Google suspending AI Pro/Ultra users for using OpenClaw/OpenCode, which commenters described as draconian and zero-tolerance [E5][E6].

evidence.summary: The Claude Code workflow post generated strong practical interest, but one detailed top comment said cheaper Codex and Gemini setups were faster and less prone to context exhaustion than Claude Code.
In the AI-assisted binary-audit thread, Claude Opus 4.6 was cited as finding more backdoors than GPT, but with a 22% false-positive rate, making the signal mixed rather than clearly favorable.
A top commenter in the Claude Code thread said their Codex-based implementer workflow was quicker, cheaper, and had fewer context stall-outs than Claude Code.
The binary-audit discussion explicitly called GPT impressive for maintaining a 0% false-positive rate across models, even though recall topped out at 18%.
In the Claude Code workflow thread, a commenter said they used Gemini Pro for planning and that a $20 Gemini plan compared favorably on speed and cost.
The Google AI Pro/Ultra restriction story drew strong criticism after users reported suspensions for using OpenClaw/OpenCode, with commenters calling the policy draconian and zero-tolerance.
